physics.Newton's Laws: 1st: object stays at rest/motion unless acted upon. 2nd: F=ma (force = mass × acceleration). 3rd: every action has equal and opposite reaction.
physics.Thermodynamics: 1st: energy conserved. 2nd: entropy always increases (heat flows hot→cold). 3rd: absolute zero (-273.15°C) is unattainable. Laws govern all heat/energy processes.
physics.Relativity (Special): Einstein 1905. Speed of light constant in all frames. E=mc² (mass-energy equivalence). Time dilation: moving clocks tick slower. Length contraction: moving objects shorten.
physics.Relativity (General): Einstein 1915. Gravity = curvature of spacetime by mass. Predicts: black holes, gravitational waves (LIGO 2015 detected), time dilation near massive objects, Mercury's orbital precession.
physics.Quantum Mechanics: Particles are probability waves until measured (wavefunction collapse). Uncertainty principle (Δx·Δp ≥ ħ/2): can't know position and momentum simultaneously. Quantized energy levels.
physics.Electromagnetism: Maxwell's 4 equations unify electricity and magnetism. Light = electromagnetic wave. Moving charges create magnetic fields, changing magnetic fields create electric fields.
physics.Standard Model: Particle physics model. Matter particles: quarks (6 types) + leptons (6 types, including electron). Force carriers: photon (EM), gluons (strong), W/Z bosons (weak), graviton (hypothetical).
physics.Conservation Laws: Energy, momentum, angular momentum, electric charge, color charge, baryon number, lepton number. Noether's theorem: every symmetry has a conserved quantity.
physics.Wave-Particle Duality: Everything exhibits both wave and particle properties. Photons: double-slit experiment shows interference (wave), photoelectric effect shows particle nature. de Broglie: λ = h/p.
physics.Nuclear Fusion: Atomic nuclei combine, releasing energy. Powers stars (hydrogen → helium). Requires extreme temperature/pressure. Tokamaks (ITER) aim for sustained fusion on Earth.
physics.Nuclear Fission: Heavy nucleus splits into lighter ones, releasing energy. Chain reaction: neutrons trigger more splits. Used in nuclear power plants and atomic bombs. Critical mass required.
physics.Entropy: Measure of disorder. Second law: total entropy of isolated system always increases. Statistical interpretation: more microstates means higher entropy. Low entropy = organized, high entropy = random.

chemistry.Periodic Table: 118 elements organized by atomic number. Periods (rows): electron shells. Groups (columns): similar chemical properties. Metals left, nonmetals right, metalloids in between.
chemistry.Chemical Bonds: Ionic: electron transfer (Na+Cl-). Covalent: electron sharing (H₂O). Metallic: delocalized electrons (metal lattice). Hydrogen bonds: weak, between H and O/N/F.
chemistry.States of Matter: Solid (fixed shape/volume), liquid (fixed volume, variable shape), gas (variable both), plasma (ionized gas, stars). Phase changes: melting, freezing, boiling, condensation, sublimation.
chemistry.Acids and Bases: Acids donate H+ (pH < 7): HCl, H₂SO₄. Bases accept H+ (pH > 7): NaOH, NH₃. Neutral at pH 7. Strong acids/bases fully dissociate. Buffer solutions resist pH change.
chemistry.Organic Chemistry: Carbon-based compounds. Functional groups: alcohols (-OH), carboxylic acids (-COOH), amines (-NH₂). Hydrocarbons: alkanes (single bonds), alkenes (double), alkynes (triple).
chemistry.Stoichiometry: Quantitative relationships in reactions. Balanced equations: 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O. Moles: Avogadro's number (6.022×10²³). Limiting reagent determines product amount.
chemistry.Electrochemistry: Redox reactions: oxidation (lose electrons) + reduction (gain electrons). Batteries convert chemical→electrical. Electrolysis uses electricity to drive non-spontaneous reactions.
chemistry.Catalysis: Substances that speed up reactions without being consumed. Enzymes (biological catalysts). Heterogeneous (different phase) vs homogeneous (same phase). Lower activation energy.

biology.Cell Theory: All living things composed of cells. Cell is basic unit of life. All cells arise from existing cells. Two types: prokaryotic (no nucleus, bacteria) and eukaryotic (has nucleus, plants/animals/fungi).
biology.DNA: Double helix, stores genetic information. Nucleotides: A (adenine) pairs with T (thymine), G (guanine) with C (cytosine). Genes code for proteins via transcription→translation.
biology.Evolution by Natural Selection: Darwin 1859. Variation exists in populations. Some traits give survival/reproduction advantage. Advantageous traits become more common over generations. Speciation: accumulation of changes → new species.
biology.Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Plants convert sunlight to chemical energy. Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll (green pigment). Two phases: light-dependent (ATP/NADPH) and Calvin cycle (fix CO₂).
biology.Cellular Respiration: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ATP. Glucose broken down to produce energy (ATP). Stages: glycolysis (cytoplasm), Krebs cycle (mitochondria), electron transport chain (mitochondria).
biology.Central Dogma: DNA → RNA → Protein. DNA replicated (cell division). DNA transcribed to mRNA (nucleus). mRNA translated to protein (ribosomes). Reverse transcription (retroviruses like HIV).
biology.Ecosystems: Biotic (living) + abiotic (non-living) factors. Food chains: producer → primary consumer → secondary consumer → tertiary consumer. Energy flow: ~10% transfers between trophic levels.
biology.Human Body Systems: Nervous (brain, neurons), circulatory (heart, blood vessels), respiratory (lungs, trachea), digestive (stomach, intestines), immune (lymphocytes, antibodies), endocrine (hormones), skeletal, muscular.

mathematics.Algebra: Study of symbols and rules for manipulating them. Linear equations (ax + b = 0), quadratics (ax² + bx + c = 0 → x = [-b ± √(b²-4ac)]/2a), systems of equations, polynomials.
mathematics.Calculus: Study of change. Derivative (rate of change, slope): d/dx x² = 2x. Integral (area under curve): ∫f(x)dx. Fundamental theorem: differentiation and integration are inverses.
mathematics.Linear Algebra: Study of vector spaces and linear transformations. Vectors, matrices, dot product, eigenvalues/eigenvectors. Used everywhere: ML (data as vectors), graphics (transformation matrices), physics.
mathematics.Probability: Measure of likelihood. P(A) = outcomes favoring A / total outcomes. Conditional: P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B). Bayes' theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B). Distributions: normal, binomial, Poisson.
mathematics.Statistics: Collecting, analyzing, interpreting data. Mean (average), median (middle), mode (most common). Standard deviation (spread). Hypothesis testing (p-value, confidence intervals). Correlation ≠ causation.
mathematics.Number Theory: Properties of integers. Primes (divisible only by 1 and itself), GCD/LCM, modular arithmetic (clock arithmetic), Fermat's little theorem, RSA encryption based on prime factorization difficulty.
mathematics.Graph Theory: Study of graphs (nodes + edges). Eulerian paths (visit every edge once), Hamiltonian paths (visit every node once). Trees (connected, acyclic). Used in networks, routing, scheduling.
mathematics.Set Theory: Collections of objects. Union (A ∪ B: all in either), intersection (A ∩ B: in both), complement (not in A). Venn diagrams. Cardinality (size). ZFC axioms: foundation of modern mathematics.
mathematics.Trigonometry: Relations between angles and sides of triangles. sin(θ) = opposite/hypotenuse, cos(θ) = adjacent/hypotenuse, tan(θ) = opposite/adjacent. Unit circle. Used in waves, rotation, periodic phenomena.

computing.Turing Machine: Theoretical model of computation. Infinite tape, read/write head, state machine. Anything computable can be computed by a Turing machine (Church-Turing thesis).
computing.Boolean Logic: True/false operations. AND (both true), OR (at least one true), NOT (invert), XOR (exactly one true). De Morgan's laws. Basis of digital circuits and all computing.
computing.Binary: Base-2 number system. Bit (0 or 1), Byte (8 bits). Represent everything: integers (two's complement), text (ASCII, Unicode), images (RGB pixels), instructions (CPU opcodes).
computing.Von Neumann Architecture: CPU (control unit + ALU) + memory + I/O. Stored-program concept: instructions and data in same memory. Fetch-decode-execute cycle. Bottleneck: memory slower than CPU.
computing.Time Complexity: Measure of how runtime grows with input size. O(1) constant, O(log n) logarithmic, O(n) linear, O(n log n) linearithmic, O(n²) quadratic, O(2ⁿ) exponential. Big-O ignores constants and lower terms.
computing.Compiler vs Interpreter: Compiler translates entire program to machine code before execution (C, Rust, Go). Interpreter executes line by line (Python, JS, Ruby). JIT compiles hot paths at runtime (Java, C#).
computing.Operating System: Manages hardware, processes, memory, files, devices. Kernel (core, privileged), user space (applications). Types: monolithic (Linux), microkernel (Minix), hybrid (Windows NT).
computing.Virtual Memory: Each process gets virtual address space (4GB on 32-bit). MMU maps virtual→physical pages. Pages swapped to disk when RAM full. Pros: isolation, overcommit, simplified programming.
computing.CPU Architecture: CISC (complex instructions, x86): variable length, many addressing modes. RISC (reduced, ARM): fixed length, load-store architecture. Pipelining: execute multiple instructions in stages.
computing.Database Index: Data structure for fast lookups. B-tree: balanced, O(log n) for insert/update/delete. Hash index: O(1) equal lookups, no range queries. Composite index: multiple columns, leftmost prefix rule.

space.Solar System: Sun (star) + 8 planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars (terrestrial); Jupiter, Saturn (gas giants); Uranus, Neptune (ice giants). Asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter. Kuiper belt beyond Neptune.
space.Stars: Fusion-powered balls of plasma. Lifecycle: nebula → protostar → main sequence → red giant → white dwarf/neutron star/black hole (depending on mass). Hertzsprung-Russell diagram: luminosity vs temperature.
space.Galaxies: Gravitationally bound systems of stars, gas, dust, dark matter. Types: spiral (Milky Way, Andromeda), elliptical, irregular. Milky Way: ~100 billion stars, ~100,000 light-years across.
space.Black Holes: Region where gravity prevents anything (even light) from escaping. Event horizon: point of no return. Singularity: infinite density. Types: stellar-mass (few solar masses), supermassive (millions/billions, at galaxy centers).
space.Big Bang: Universe began ~13.8 billion years ago from infinitely hot, dense point. Evidence: cosmic microwave background radiation, Hubble's law (galaxies moving away), element abundances (H, He, Li).
space.Dark Matter: Invisible matter that doesn't interact with EM radiation (no light, no reflection). Detected via gravitational effects: galaxy rotation curves (outer stars move too fast), gravitational lensing. ~27% of universe.
space.Dark Energy: Mysterious force causing universe's expansion to accelerate. ~68% of universe. Einstein's cosmological constant (repulsive gravity). Nature unknown: quintessence? modified gravity? vacuum energy?
